era: Indie Rock. E=6 -- power trio garage rock. Three musicians, maximum volume. Sub Pop returns. The Sonics lineage. v1.49.346.

location.formative: Seattle garage rock tradition. The Sonics (1960s), Hendrix, grunge, power trio lineage.
location.career: Naked Giants. 2013-present. Sub Pop. Power trio garage rock.

members.name: Grant Mullen
members.note: One guitar filling the space of two. Thick fuzz distortion. The power trio demand.

members.name: Gianni Aiello
members.note: Co-lead instrument. Fat, distorted, melodic. Not support -- lead.

members.name: Henry LaVallee
members.note: Engine and anchor. Aggressive, dynamic. Sets the pace.

theory_nodes.concept: First power trio in the engine -- three musicians generating E=6. Every instrument essential. No hiding behind layers. The format is a trust exercise.
examples: Naked Giants: guitar, bass, drums. Three = six.
Cream, Rush, ZZ Top: the tradition.
Economy of form: maximum output, minimum personnel.
theory_nodes.description: First power trio. Three musicians, E=6. Economy of form as aesthetic principle.

genealogy.relationship: Naked Giants at 205. First power trio in engine.

theory_nodes.concept: Sub Pop returns at 205 after Hardly Art subsidiary streak (201-204). Parent label. Different energy signature: kinetic garage vs. feminist scene-connection.
examples: Hardly Art streak: 201-204 (Chastity Belt, La Luz, Tacocat, Childbirth).
Sub Pop returns: 205 (Naked Giants). Parent label. Garage rock.
Label family: subsidiary then parent. Different curation.
theory_nodes.description: Sub Pop returns after Hardly Art. Parent label. Garage rock reset.

genealogy.relationship: Naked Giants at 205. Sub Pop parent returns.

theory_nodes.concept: Seattle garage rock lineage -- The Sonics (1960s) through Hendrix through grunge to Naked Giants. Six decades. Small bands, enormous noise. Principle transmitted, not sound.
examples: The Sonics: 1960s, Tacoma/Seattle, original PNW garage.
Hendrix Experience: Seattle-born, power trio pioneer.
Grunge: started as near-trios, garage ethos.
theory_nodes.description: Seattle garage rock: Sonics to Hendrix to grunge to Naked Giants. Six decades.

genealogy.relationship: Naked Giants at 205. The lineage continues.

cross_references.relationship: Previous E=6. Feminist supergroup vs. garage power trio. Same energy, different axis.
Three consecutive E=6 (203-205). Pop-punk, garage-raw, power trio. Three textures at one level.
Sub Pop heavyweight. Quiet restraint vs. garage force. The label's range.
ENERGY MATCH (E=6 + E=6 = 12). Impact force: volume/speed vs. 800 lbs cranial impact.
